name: ABBA Engine League
purpose: Form temporary or persistent teams from app fractions and swarm capabilities, run them against the same product objective, compare outcomes, and promote the strongest engine room configuration.
teamModel: A team is a composition of app fractions, swarm capabilities, human roles, AI roles, delivery routes, fallback routes, and proof routes.

competitionTypes.goal: deliver the same user outcome with the best speed, quality and reliability
achieve the target outcome at the lowest safe total cost
maintain service through provider or workflow failure
produce the strongest adoption, retention and ecosystem expansion pathway
produce the strongest verification, traceability and audit evidence
produce the best useful and dignified human outcome
build and operate the strongest end-to-end product engine

matchRules: All teams receive the same product brief, constraints, test data, budget ceiling and completion window.
Teams may use different app fractions from the same or different swarms.
The same app may play for multiple teams when capacity, cost and isolation rules allow.
A team must declare every dependency, provider fraction, human role, AI role and fallback route before the match.
No team may win by hiding failures, costs, human effort, unsafe data access or missing proof.
The winner is selected by weighted outcome score, not by brand popularity or number of apps used.
A winning team enters champion status only after repeat performance and live health evidence.
Losing teams are not discarded; strong fractions may be transferred, merged or retained as reserves.

leagueLevels.meaning: best current engine configuration for a product pathway

promotionRule: A team is promoted by repeated evidence across matches, not a single result.
productEngineRule: The champion team becomes the current engine room of the product, while runner-up and specialist teams remain available for fallback, comparison, regional delivery, cost optimization or future rematches.
